name: Delika Canyon Trail
name_original: Salto del río Nervión desde Delika — cascadas del Monte Santiago
description: Hike to the highest waterfall in the Iberian Peninsula, the Nervión Waterfall, with panoramic views from Monte Santiago or canyon exploratio
region_intro: Hiking around Amurrio offers diverse natural landscapes within the province of Alava, characterized by soft, green mountains and the banks of the Nervión River. The region features the impressive Sierra Salvada, a rocky range with lush forests and karst limestone formations, providing panoramic views. Nearby, the Gorbeia Natural Park contributes varied terrain with bare rock faces, rolling pastures, and extensive beech and oak forests. These features create a rich environment for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Amurrio The most popular hiking route is Delika Canyon Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the Delika Canyon, offering views of the Salto del Nervión waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salto del río Nervión – Monte Santiago Natural Monument, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This trail explores the Monte Santiago Natural Monument, providing access to viewpoints over the Nervión waterfall. Local hikers also love the Salto del Nervion — circular from Untza, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes around the waterfall, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Amurrio is defined by its soft green mountains, the Nervión River, and the dramatic karst formations of Sierra Salvada. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Amurrio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 230 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore Amurrio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Amurrio?
A: Amurrio offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 60 trails available. These routes cater to different skill levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Amurrio?
A: Hiking in Amurrio provides a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter soft, green mountains, the banks of the Nervión River, and the dramatic karst formations of the Sierra Salvada. The region also features lush forests, rolling pastures, and impressive rocky ranges, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any hikes with waterfalls in Amurrio?
A: Yes, Amurrio is home to spectacular waterfalls. The most famous is the Salto del Nervión, one of the highest waterfalls on the Iberian Peninsula. You can experience it on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056296168/delika-canyon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Delika Canyon Trail&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497794493/salto-del-rio-nervion-monte-santiago-natural-monument" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del río Nervión – Monte Santiago Natural Monument&lt;/a&gt;. Another notable cascade is the Gujuli/Goiuri waterfall, which also offers a significant drop.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Amurrio?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036035436/salto-del-nervion-circular-from-untza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Nervion — circular from Untza&lt;/a&gt; offers a varied landscape experience around the waterfall. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24639234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Pico del Fraile – Txarlazo loop from Orduña&lt;/a&gt;, which provides stunning vistas.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of hikes in Amurrio?
A: Amurrio offers a good mix of difficulty levels. You'll find approximately 19 easy routes, 38 moderate routes, and 9 more challenging trails. This variety ensures that hikers of all abilities can find a suitable path.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Amurrio?
A: The hiking trails around Amurrio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 230 reviews. Hikers often praise the breathtaking panoramic views from peaks like those in Sierra Salvada, the dramatic natural features like the Salto del Nervión, and the diverse natural beauty of the region.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on the trails?
A: Yes, the natural areas around Amurrio, particularly the Sierra Salvada, are excellent for wildlife spotting. It's a prime location for birdwatching, with species like the Egyptian vulture, peregrine falcon, and griffon vulture commonly sighted. The nearby Gorbeia Natural Park also offers opportunities to observe diverse flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Amurrio?
A: For families, the Nervión Linear Park offers interesting and generally easier trails along the Nervión River, suitable for walking or cycling. Many of the easier routes in the region, which make up about 19 of the total trails, can also be suitable for families, depending on the children's age and experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to see along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can find stunning vistas from peaks in Sierra Salvada like Tologorri, Unguino, and Txarlazo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Pico del Fraile&lt;/a&gt; offer another impressive perspective. For a truly iconic sight, the viewpoints over the Salto del Nervión are a must-see, especially after heavy rains.

Q: Is it possible to hike near the Gorbeia Natural Park from Amurrio?
A: While not directly within Amurrio, the Gorbeia Natural Park is a significant nearby attraction and a traditional reference point for Basque mountaineers. It offers a vast network of trails through varied landscapes of bare rock faces, rolling pastures, and extensive beech and oak forests. You can find more information about the park on the &lt;a href="https://www.basquecountry-tourism.com/nature-basque-country/gorbeia/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basque Country Tourism website&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in the Amurrio area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Amurrio offers several difficult routes. An epic route mentioned in the region research covers 24 km from Amurrio to Orduña, passing through Tologorri and Txarlazo, with a significant 1,200m elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31861895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Santiago Wolf Trap – Nervión River loop from Artomaña&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option, covering over 19 km with substantial elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Amurrio?
A: The region's soft, green mountains suggest that spring and autumn are ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. The Salto del Nervión waterfall is particularly spectacular after heavy rains, making late autumn or spring a great time to witness its full glory.
tips: The succession of small waterfalls of the Nervión River is truly amazing, a spectacle. This viewpoint is very close to the El Infierno restaurant. Essential!
This beautiful bridge crosses the Nervión River, in the middle of the Delika canyon.
As its name suggests, they are steps of waterfalls that, with water, are a joy to see and photograph.
This spectacular waterfall can only be seen right after heavy rain or during thaw.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Nervión Waterfall?
faqs.answer: The Nervión Waterfall is highly seasonal. It is most impressive during late winter and early spring, or after significant rainfall and snowmelt. During summer, it may be dry, but the canyon views from both above and below are still considered well worth the visit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: There are multiple access points with various parking options available. Depending on whether you want to view the waterfall from above (Monte Santiago) or below (Delika), you'll find parking areas designed to shorten your walk to the viewpoints. The roads leading to the area are scenic.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trails in the Monte Santiago Natural Monument area are generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, to protect the local wildlife and for the safety of other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike here?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit or to pay an entrance fee to access the trails around the Nervión Waterfall within the Monte Santiago Natural Monument. It is a natural area open to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The region features a diverse karst landscape with rugged cliffs, deep canyons, and lush forests. Depending on your chosen path, you might encounter well-marked trails through beech groves and oak forests from Monte Santiago, or follow a river path with potential crossings through the dramatic geological formations of the Delika canyon.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights I can see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers spectacular views, including the impressive Nervión Waterfall itself, which is the highest in the Iberian Peninsula. You can also discover the Viewpoint over the Horses' Pool, the Zalborondo Bridge, and the Terraced Waterfalls.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: The difficulty varies by route. The 'Senda Verde de Delika' from below is generally described as a beautiful and simple route, suitable for families. However, an 8km round trip through the Delika Canyon is considered moderate and may involve river crossings, which could be challenging for younger children or those with limited fitness.

faqs.question: What is the difference between approaching from Monte Santiago versus Delika?
faqs.answer: Approaching from Monte Santiago provides panoramic views of the waterfall's dramatic drop from above. The trails here are often well-marked and offer vistas of the Sierra Salvada. The Delika approach, via the 'Senda Verde de Delika', takes you into the canyon from below, offering a unique perspective of the water cascading above and dramatic geological formations, though it may involve river crossings.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for river crossings, especially if hiking through the Delika canyon, waterproof footwear is highly advisable. Layered clothing is recommended due to changing weather conditions, and always bring enough water and snacks. Trekking poles can also be helpful on some sections.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers about 10% of the official trail known as Delika Entitatea. You may encounter sections of this and other local paths as you explore the area around the Nervión Waterfall.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this trail?
faqs.answer: Be aware that the waterfall's flow is highly seasonal; if hiking through the Delika canyon during peak flow, reaching the end of the route from below might be challenging due to river levels. Always check local conditions and weather forecasts before heading out, especially during wet seasons, as paths can become slippery.
